name: Point SOP
description: The Point SOP allows you to get right into the geometry and manipulate the position, color, texture coordinates, and normals of the points in the Source, and other attributes. The Point SOP also lets you create custom point attributes. It is the complement to the Primitive SOP. Using a second input allows for combining of two SOPs using their respective expressions (see: PointSOP Class). If the second input has less points than the first input, the points in the second input will be cycled.

parameters.name: Position
parameters.label: Position
parameters.description:
t -  - Expressions to translate the XYZ coordinates of a given point can be entered here. The attributes to modify here are: ,  and .	
Simply entering  into the Position X field means that the X coordinate of each point that comes in is passed straight through with no modification.			
Changing this entry to  means that the X coordinate of each point that comes in will be displaced by 5 units. This expression can be expanded to produce many useful effects. Transformations can also be effected in the Y and Z fields.

parameters.name: Normals

parameters.description:
n -  - If you select 'Add Normal' from the menu above, N normal attributes will be added/modified in the . Enter expressions to change a given point normal here.  normals are directional vectors used by other SOPs, such as Turbulence, Facet and Copy. See Attributes article for detailed information. The attributes to modify are: ,  and .
If you select 'No Normal' from the menu above, the N normal attribute will be removed from the .

parameters.name: Width(Line MAT)
parameters.label: Width(Line MAT)
parameters.description:
dowidth -  - Select between keeping the width attribute or adding a new width. This Width (width) attribute is used exclusively with Line MAT to control line width when the material is rendered.

 off - on -

parameters.name: Keep Width
parameters.label: Keep Width
parameters.description: off - on -

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
dopscale -  - Select between keeping the scale attribute, adding new scale, or using no scale. This scale (pscale) attribute is used with the Particle SOP and acts as a multiplier for the size of particles. The value of this attribute is multiplied by the size specified in the Particle SOP's render attributes to scale each particle. This attribute is used by the Point Sprite MAT when rendering point sprites.

 off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Keep Scale
parameters.label: Keep Scale
parameters.description: off - on - remove -

parameters.description: dotension - Tension affects the elasticity of the edges the point is connected to.

parameters.name: Up Vector
parameters.label: Up Vector
parameters.description:
doup -  - Creates/Removes the "up" attribute for points. This attribute defines an up vector which is used to fully define the space around a point (for particle instancing or copying geometry). The up vector can be used in conjunction with the copy template's normals to control the orientation of the copies in the Copy SOP.

 off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Keep Up Vector
parameters.label: Keep Up Vector
parameters.description: off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.label: Radius
parameters.description:
doradius -  - Retains, adds, or removes radiusf attributes for points, used to modify the distance roll-off effect. The roll-off is: r /(r+d^2) Where r is radius, and d is distance from attractor point. If no radius is set, no attenuation is performed.

 off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Keep Radius
parameters.label: Keep Radius
parameters.description: off - on - remove -

parameters.name: F Scale
parameters.label: F Scale
parameters.description:
doscale -  - Retains, adds, or removes scalef attributes for points, a multiplier for total force associated with this attractor point.	
Both Radius and Force Scale will default to 1 if not created as point attributes.			
Radial / Normal / Edge / Directional Force - These four parameters introduce a type of force when created and each has a corresponding multiplier associated with it.


 off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Keep F Scale
parameters.label: Keep F Scale
parameters.description: off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Radial F
parameters.label: Radial F
parameters.description:
doradialf -  - Retains, adds, or removes radialf attributes for points, the force directed towards the attractor point. Positive multipliers are towards while negative are away.

 off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Keep Radial F
parameters.label: Keep Radial F
parameters.description: off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Normal F
parameters.label: Normal F
parameters.description:
donormalf -  - Retains, adds, or removes normalf attributes for points, the force directed along the point normal direction.

 off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Keep Normal F
parameters.label: Keep Normal F
parameters.description: off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Edge F
parameters.label: Edge F
parameters.description:
doedgef -  - Retains, adds, or removes edgef attributes for points, which only works on primitive face types. The force is directed in the direction of the edge leading from that point. If multiple vertices reference the same point, then the direction is the edge direction of the last primitive referencing the point.	
If the face open, then the end point has an edge direction equal to that of the preceding point in that primitive.			
Note: When edge forces are added using the  , the force directions are computed in the   itself. Thus, any following transformations do not effect these. If you wish for the edge directions to be transformed as well, all transformations must be done before the  . Only the edge forces function like this.


 off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Keep Edge F
parameters.label: Keep Edge F
parameters.description: off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Dir. F
parameters.label: Dir. F
parameters.description:
dodirf -  - Retains, adds, or removes dirf attributes for points, an arbitrary directional force still affected by the distance roll-off function.

 off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Keep Dir. F
parameters.label: Keep Dir. F
parameters.description: off - on - remove -
